title: castilleja rubicundula subsp. lithospermoides
intro: Castilleja rubicundula subsp. lithospermoides, also known as the rock-pink or mountain Indian paintbrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Castilleja rubicundula subsp. lithospermoides include its potential to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of this plant may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to soothe sore throats and calm digestive issues.
